title: The Services (Temporary Powers) Ordinance, 1963 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
title_en: The Services (Temporary Powers) Ordinance, 1963 (East Pakistan Ordinance)
description: An Ordinance to provide for powers for the maintenance of services and supplies essential to the life of the community and for the maintenance of public order. WHEREAS it is necessary to provide for powers for the maintenance of services and supplies essential to the life of the community and for the maintenance of public order; AND WHEREAS the Provincial Assembly of East Pakistan is not in session and the Governo...

provisions.title: 1. Short title, extent and commencement
provisions.content:
1. (1) This Ordinance may be called the [* * *] Services (Temporary Powers) Ordinance, 1963.
(2) It extends to the whole of [Bangladesh].
(3) It shall come into force at once.

provisions.title: 2. Prohibition of strikes, etc., by employees
provisions.content: 2. Whenever the Government is satisfied that, in the interest of public order or for the maintenance of services or supplies essential to the life of the community, it is necessary to prohibit any strike by any employee of the Government or [of any body or establishment], the Government may, by notification in the official Gazette, prohibit any strike for a period of three months from the date of the notification.

provisions.title: 3. Penalty
provisions.content:
3. (1) Notwithstanding anything contained in [the Industrial Relations Ordinance, 1969 (Ord. No. XXIII of 1969)], or in any other law for the time being in force, any strike, preparation, propagation or organisation for strike or cessation of work or abandonment of work [by any employee] during the period specified in section 2 shall be punishable with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year or with fine of Taka one thousand or with both.
(2) Any person who abets, instigates or incites others to take part in, or otherwise acts in furtherance of, a strike, shall be punishable with rigorous imprisonment for a term which may extend to one year, or with fine of Taka one thousand or with both.

provisions.title: 4. Jurisdiction and mode of trial
provisions.content:
4. (1) No Magistrate other than a Magistrate of the first class shall try an offence under this Ordinance.
(2) Notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 , an offence under this Ordinance shall be cognizable.
(3) Notwithstanding anything contained in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 , a Magistrate trying an offence under this Ordinance, shall have power to try such offence summarily and in trying such offences such Magistrates shall follow the procedure laid down in the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 .

provisions.title: 5. [Omitted]
provisions.content: 5. [Cognizance of offences.- Omitted by section 2 of the East Pakistan Services (Temporary Powers) (Amendment) Ordinance, 1970 (East Pakistan Ordinance No. XXII of 1970).]
